raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":1.0,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Making the section stronger requires reframing the central claim about disagreement as a quality signal in light of what the reported results actually imply.","R1.2":"The delegation centrally requires checking the argument chain because the reported negative correlations appear to conflict with the claim that higher disagreement predicts higher translation quality.","R1.3":"The section's strength depends on whether the stated claims about reliability, robustness, and practitioner use are supported by the reported COMET, chrF, BLEU, and language-pair correlations.","R1.6":"The broad request to make the passage stronger includes improving sentence-level clarity, force, and concision once the substantive issues are addressed.","RX.1":"Any revision responsibility would need to disclose uncertainty around interpreting correlations, sample scope, and causal mechanisms.","RX.2":"Overclaim avoidance is load-bearing because the artifact makes strong claims about reliability, robustness, and deployment recommendations from correlational evidence.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within strengthening the paper passage rather than executing new experiments or changing the underlying study.","RX.4":"The deployment recommendation affects human review decisions, so avoiding downstream misuse of a weak or inverted signal is an active responsibility.","RX.5":"The strengthened version must preserve traceability from each claim to the reported metrics, sample, and language-pair breakdown."}}

rationale.R1.1: Making the section stronger requires reframing the central claim about disagreement as a quality signal in light of what the reported results actually imply.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation centrally requires checking the argument chain because the reported negative correlations appear to conflict with the claim that higher disagreement predicts higher translation quality.
rationale.R1.3: The section's strength depends on whether the stated claims about reliability, robustness, and practitioner use are supported by the reported COMET, chrF, BLEU, and language-pair correlations.
rationale.R1.6: The broad request to make the passage stronger includes improving sentence-level clarity, force, and concision once the substantive issues are addressed.
rationale.RX.1: Any revision responsibility would need to disclose uncertainty around interpreting correlations, sample scope, and causal mechanisms.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is load-bearing because the artifact makes strong claims about reliability, robustness, and deployment recommendations from correlational evidence.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within strengthening the paper passage rather than executing new experiments or changing the underlying study.
rationale.RX.4: The deployment recommendation affects human review decisions, so avoiding downstream misuse of a weak or inverted signal is an active responsibility.
rationale.RX.5: The strengthened version must preserve traceability from each claim to the reported metrics, sample, and language-pair breakdown.
